description: Extend per-user filesystem isolation to uploads, artifacts, thread directories, MCP config, and fix minor user_id leaks in memory/status and assistants_compat.

userStories.title: Paths: add user-scoped thread directory methods
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want Paths to expose user-scoped thread directory helpers and resolve helpers with user_id=None fallback, so that uploads/outputs/workspace can be stored under users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/.
acceptanceCriteria: Paths.user_thread_dir(user_id, thread_id) returns {base_dir}/users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/
Paths.user_thread_uploads_dir(user_id, thread_id) returns {base_dir}/users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/uploads/
Paths.user_thread_outputs_dir(user_id, thread_id) returns {base_dir}/users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/outputs/
Paths.user_thread_workspace_dir(user_id, thread_id) returns {base_dir}/users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/workspace/
Paths.resolve_uploads_dir(thread_id, user_id=None) returns user-scoped path when user_id given, else sandbox_uploads_dir(thread_id)
Paths.resolve_outputs_dir(thread_id, user_id=None) returns user-scoped path when user_id given, else sandbox_outputs_dir(thread_id)
Paths.resolve_workspace_dir(thread_id, user_id=None) returns user-scoped path when user_id given, else sandbox_work_dir(thread_id)
Paths.resolve_thread_user_data_dir(thread_id, user_id=None) returns user-scoped thread dir when user_id given, else sandbox_user_data_dir(thread_id)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Paths: user-scoped ensure_thread_dirs, delete_thread_dir, resolve_virtual_path, host_sandbox_* methods
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want ensure_thread_dirs, delete_thread_dir, resolve_virtual_path, and host_sandbox_* methods to accept an optional user_id so that directory creation, deletion, virtual path resolution, and Docker volume mounts all use the correct user-scoped physical path.
acceptanceCriteria: ensure_thread_dirs(thread_id, user_id=None) creates workspace/uploads/outputs under users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/ when user_id is given
ensure_thread_dirs(thread_id) with no user_id behaves exactly as before
delete_thread_dir(thread_id, user_id=None) deletes users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/ when user_id is given
delete_thread_dir(thread_id) with no user_id behaves exactly as before
resolve_virtual_path(thread_id, virtual_path, user_id=None) resolves to user-scoped physical path when user_id is given
resolve_virtual_path(thread_id, virtual_path) with no user_id behaves exactly as before
host_sandbox_uploads_dir, host_sandbox_outputs_dir, host_sandbox_work_dir, host_sandbox_user_data_dir each accept optional user_id and return user-scoped host path when provided
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ThreadDataMiddleware + UploadsMiddleware: pass user_id to path resolution
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want ThreadDataMiddleware and UploadsMiddleware to extract user_id from run metadata and use user-scoped paths, so that thread directories and upload file listings are in the correct user namespace.
acceptanceCriteria: ThreadDataMiddleware.before_agent extracts user_id from runtime.context.get('user_id') first, then config metadata as fallback (same pattern as MemoryMiddleware)
ThreadDataMiddleware calls ensure_thread_dirs(thread_id, user_id=user_id)
ThreadDataMiddleware._get_thread_paths uses resolve_uploads_dir / resolve_outputs_dir / resolve_workspace_dir with user_id
UploadsMiddleware.before_agent extracts user_id from runtime.context or config metadata
UploadsMiddleware resolves uploads_dir via paths.resolve_uploads_dir(thread_id, user_id) instead of paths.sandbox_uploads_dir(thread_id)
When user_id is None in both middlewares, behavior is identical to before
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: uploads.py router: use user-scoped uploads directory
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want my uploaded files stored under users/{user_id}/threads/{thread_id}/uploads/ so that my files are isolated from other users.
acceptanceCriteria: All three endpoints (POST upload, GET list, DELETE file) accept request: Request and call get_optional_user_id(request)
get_uploads_dir(thread_id, user_id=None) and ensure_uploads_dir(thread_id, user_id=None) in uploads/manager.py accept optional user_id and delegate to resolve_uploads_dir
POST /api/threads/{thread_id}/uploads stores files in user-scoped path when user_id present
GET /api/threads/{thread_id}/uploads/list lists files from user-scoped path
DELETE /api/threads/{thread_id}/uploads/{filename} deletes from user-scoped path
sandbox_uploads path returned in response uses resolve_uploads_dir result
When user_id is None, all endpoints behave exactly as before
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: artifacts.py + path_utils.py: use user-scoped virtual path resolution
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want GET /api/threads/{thread_id}/artifacts/{path} to resolve artifact paths from my user-scoped directory so that I can only access my own artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: path_utils.resolve_thread_virtual_path(thread_id, virtual_path, user_id=None) passes user_id to Paths.resolve_virtual_path
artifacts.py get_artifact endpoint accepts request: Request and calls get_optional_user_id(request)
get_artifact passes user_id to resolve_thread_virtual_path for both normal paths and .skill archive paths
When user_id is None, behavior is identical to before
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: mcp.py: make PUT /api/mcp/config read-only, add per-user enabled toggle
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want to toggle individual MCP servers on/off for myself without affecting other users, and the global MCP config should be read-only.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/mcp/config returns 403 with detail message directing users to the new per-user endpoint
GET /api/mcp/config merges global MCP server list with per-user enabled overrides from users/{user_id}/extensions_config.json when user is logged in
New endpoint PUT /api/mcp/servers/{name}/enabled exists with request body {enabled: bool}
PUT /api/mcp/servers/{name}/enabled requires auth (returns 401 if not logged in)
PUT /api/mcp/servers/{name}/enabled returns 404 if server name not in global config
PUT /api/mcp/servers/{name}/enabled writes only the enabled field to users/{user_id}/extensions_config.json mcpServers section, preserving existing skills entries
GET /api/mcp/config without auth returns global config unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix memory/status endpoint missing user_id
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want GET /api/memory/status to return my own memory data, not the global fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: get_memory_status() accepts request: Request parameter
get_memory_status() calls get_optional_user_id(request) and passes user_id to get_memory_data(user_id)
When not logged in, user_id is None and behavior is unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix assistants_compat missing user_id in list_custom_agents
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want the assistants list to show only my own custom agents, not all users' agents.
acceptanceCriteria: _list_assistants(user_id=None) accepts user_id parameter and passes it to list_custom_agents(user_id=user_id)
search_assistants endpoint extracts user_id from request via get_optional_user_id and passes to _list_assistants
get_assistant_compat endpoint extracts user_id from request and passes to _list_assistants
get_assistant_graph and get_assistant_schemas also pass user_id to _list_assistants
When not logged in, user_id is None and behavior is unchanged (shows global agents)
Typecheck passes
